glossary.def: The number of linearly independent rows (equivalently, columns) of a matrix — the count of genuinely distinct patterns it contains. A rank-r matrix can be written exactly as a product of an m×r and an r×n matrix, so it carries only r(m+n) degrees of freedom instead of mn.

The factorization A = UΣVᵀ that exists for every real matrix, with U and V orthogonal and Σ diagonal holding the non-negative singular values in descending order. It expresses A as an ordered sum of rank-1 layers σᵢuᵢvᵢᵀ, most important first.

The diagonal entries σ₁ ≥ σ₂ ≥ … ≥ 0 of Σ in the SVD — the factors by which the matrix stretches the unit sphere along its principal axes. They are square roots of the eigenvalues of AᵀA, hence always non-negative; their squares σᵢ² are the energy (variance) each component carries.

Keeping only the top-k singular values and vectors to form the rank-k matrix Aₖ = Σᵢ₌₁ᵏ σᵢuᵢvᵢᵀ. By Eckart–Young this is the best possible rank-k approximation in Frobenius and spectral norm, with leftover error equal to the square root of the discarded energy.

The result that the truncated SVD Aₖ minimizes ‖A − B‖ over all matrices B of rank ≤ k, with error √(Σᵢ>ₖ σᵢ²). It is what makes 'keep the top k singular values' provably optimal rather than a heuristic, and it underwrites both PCA and low-rank weight adaptation.

A recommender that assigns each user a vector pᵤ and each item a vector qᵢ in a shared k-dimensional space and predicts a rating as r̂ᵤᵢ = μ + bᵤ + bᵢ + pᵤᵀqᵢ. The dot product is the personalized interaction; the latent vectors are exactly the rows of U and V from the factorization, learned on observed entries only.

Matrix factorization fit by gradient descent on only the observed entries of a sparse ratings matrix, minimizing regularized squared error. Named for Simon Funk's Netflix-Prize method, it sidesteps plain SVD's need for a complete matrix and is still the textbook collaborative-filtering algorithm.

Factoring a non-negative matrix as A ≈ WH with both factors constrained to be ≥ 0. Because nothing can be subtracted, the data must be built as a sum of parts, so the factors come out interpretable — strokes, facial features, or text topics — fit by multiplicative update rules that preserve non-negativity automatically.

The fraction Σᵢ≤ₖ σᵢ² / Σ σᵢ² of total squared singular value kept by a rank-k approximation — identical to PCA's explained-variance ratio. Plotting it against k (the cumulative scree curve) and cutting at the elbow is the standard way to choose how many components to keep.

refs.cite: Koren, Y., Bell, R. & Volinsky, C. (2009). Matrix Factorization Techniques for Recommender Systems. IEEE Computer 42(8).
Lee, D. D. & Seung, H. S. (1999). Learning the parts of objects by non-negative matrix factorization. Nature 401.
Eckart, C. & Young, G. (1936). The approximation of one matrix by another of lower rank. Psychometrika 1(3).
Lee, D. D. & Seung, H. S. (2001). Algorithms for Non-negative Matrix Factorization. NIPS 13.
Halko, N., Martinsson, P.-G. & Tropp, J. A. (2011). Finding Structure with Randomness: Probabilistic Algorithms for Constructing Approximate Matrix Decompositions. SIAM Review 53(2).
Deerwester, S., Dumais, S. T., Furnas, G. W., Landauer, T. K. & Harshman, R. (1990). Indexing by Latent Semantic Analysis. JASIS 41(6).
Levy, O. & Goldberg, Y. (2014). Neural Word Embedding as Implicit Matrix Factorization. NIPS 27.
